title: Allow containers access to `io_uring` syscalls
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When running `mariadb:latest` container from `docker.io` image registry, it can not access `io_uring` syscalls.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Download and launch the `mariadb:latest` container from `docker.io` image registry and launch it.

2. When you check the container's logs, you'll notice that it can not access `io_uring` syscalls.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ podman logs mariadb
...
[Warning] mysqld: io_uring_queue_init() failed with ENOSYS: check seccomp filters, and the kernel version (newer than 5.1 required)
[Warning] InnoDB: liburing disabled: falling back to innodb_use_native_aio=OFF
...
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Allow the container access to `io_uring` syscalls.

Is it possible? I have already tried to search for such information however with no luck.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The issue happens every time the container is launched.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      4.3.1

Git Commit:   814b7b003cc630bf6ab188274706c383f9fb9915-dirty

```

$ uname -a
Linux <hostname> 5.19.8-1-aarch64-ARCH #1 SMP PREEMPT Thu Sep 8 18:20:33 MDT 2022 aarch64 GNU/Linux

comments.body:
It's probably the seccomp filter. Does it work running with Docker?

For Podman, you can try running with `--security-opt seccomp=unconfined`.  That will disable the seccomp filter, so be aware that it's less secure.  You can create custom seccomp profiles with https://github.com/containers/oci-seccomp-bpf-hook.

Cc: @giuseppe

I just checked the `/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json` file on my drive and compared it to `docker`'s [`default.json` file](https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/master/profiles/seccomp/default.json). It seems that there are some differences. Specifically, when it comes to `io_uring`, `docker` allows the following syscalls, but `podman` does not:

```
io_uring_enter
io_uring_register
io_uring_setup
```

Please, is it somehow possible to tell `podman` to allow these syscalls on top of its default seccomp profile? E.g. something like:

```sh
--security-opt=default+allow_additional_syscalls:io_uring_enter,io_uring_register,io_uring_setup
```

Or create a seccomp profile which inherits from / includes the default seccomp profile and allows additional syscalls on top? E.g. something like:

```
#include /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json

allow_syscalls: {
  io_uring_enter
  io_uring_register
  io_uring_setup
}
```

I want to avoid creating a new seccomp profile from scratch if possible.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Since we do not have a good solution for this, and consider this syscalls to dangerous to allow by default, you need to disable seccomp filtering or build your own seccomp.
Closing won't fix.
